resources.string: Learn the building blocks of any program. Understand how to declare variables using val and var, and explore basic types like Int, String, Boolean, and Double.
Master decision-making in code. Practice using if/else and the powerful when expression to control the flow of your program based on conditions.
Learn how to write reusable blocks of code. Understand parameters, return types, and why breaking your code into functions makes it cleaner and easier to maintain.
Discover how to repeat actions efficiently using for loops, while loops, and the repeat function. Learn how to iterate over ranges and collections.
Understand one of Kotlin\'s greatest strengths — null safety. Learn how to handle nullable types using the ? operator, ?: Elvis operator, and !! with care.
Dive into object-oriented programming. Learn how to model real-world things using classes, create objects from them, and define properties and behaviors.
Learn how classes can share behavior through inheritance and interfaces. Understand the open keyword and when to use abstract classes vs interfaces.
Explore Kotlin\'s functional side. Learn how to write short, powerful functions as expressions and pass them as arguments to other functions.
Learn how to group related data together. Understand the difference between read-only List and MutableList, and practice common operations like add, remove, and filter.
Go beyond lists. Discover Map for key-value pairs and Set for unique collections. Learn when to use each and how to query them effectively.
Deepen your functional programming skills. Explore powerful Kotlin functions like map, filter, forEach, and reduce that transform collections elegantly.
Start your UI journey. Learn what composable functions are, how the declarative UI model works, and why Compose is a major shift from the old XML approach.
Master the three fundamental layout composables. Understand how to arrange elements horizontally, vertically, and in layers using Row, Column, and Box.
Learn how Modifier is used to decorate and configure any composable. Practice setting size, padding, background, clickable, and more using the modifier chain.
Understand the heart of Compose — state. Learn how remember and mutableStateOf work together to make your UI react and update when data changes.
Go deeper on how Compose works under the hood. Understand what triggers recomposition, why it matters for performance, and how to write composables that recompose efficiently.
Learn how to display large lists efficiently. Understand why LazyColumn only renders visible items and how it differs from a regular Column.
Make your app look polished and consistent. Explore how Material 3 theming works in Compose — colors, typography, and shapes — and how to customize them.
Learn how to display images in Compose using Image composable and painterResource. Understand content scale options and how to make images look great on all screen sizes.
Discover how to group content beautifully using the Card composable. Learn how elevation and shadow create visual hierarchy in Material Design.
Add visual cues to your UI using Material icons. Learn how to use Icon and IconButton, import the extended icons library, and make your UI more intuitive.
Bring your UI to life. Learn how to add smooth animations using animateContentSize, animateDpAsState, and other simple Compose animation APIs.
Take your first step into app architecture. Understand what a ViewModel is, why it exists, and how it survives screen rotations while keeping your UI state safe.
Learn the important pattern of moving state upward. Understand why stateless composables are more reusable and testable, and how to hoist state properly.
Learn how to move between screens in a Compose app. Explore NavController, NavHost, and composable destinations to build multi-screen applications.
Understand asynchronous programming in Kotlin. Learn what coroutines are, why they replace callbacks, and how launch and suspend functions work together.
Persist data locally on the device. Get introduced to Room, Android\'s SQLite wrapper. Learn about Entity, DAO, and Database and how they work together.
Connect your app to the outside world. Learn how to make network requests using Retrofit, parse JSON responses, and display real data in your UI.
Learn how to organize your data layer cleanly. Understand the Repository pattern and how it acts as a single source of truth between your ViewModel and data sources.
Congratulations on completing 30 days of coding. You have covered Kotlin fundamentals, Jetpack Compose UI, state management, architecture, persistence, and networking. Now go build something amazing.
